free_text: Production of neutron-rich isotopes by cold
fragmentation in the reaction 197Au + Be at 950 A MeV
Erratum
Abrasion-Ablation Model.
To describe the production of extremely neutron-rich
nuclei by cold fragmentation, an analytical formulation
is presented.
Longitudinal-Momentum distributions.
The primary beam intensity was recorded
permanently by measuring the current induced in a
secondary-electron transmission monitor.
Two 5 mm thick scintillation detectors which
measured the horizontal positions at the center
and at the exit of the spectrometer as well as the
time-of-flight in the second half of the separator.
Multiple-sampling ionization chamber was mounted
at the exit in order to determine the energy loss of
the reaction products.
Corrections due to:
* ionic charge-changing reactions
* dead-time correction
* low counting rates.
All corrections were estimated to be of the
order of 20%.
The uncertainty of measurements was
determined by error of corrections and statistical
error
Correction in author' list has been done,
according to V.Zerkin remarks.
Corrections in Subents 002-034, according to new EXFOR
rules.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
ERR-T -> DATA-ERR in all Subents.
